sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.59 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Suppose that$G$is a triply transitive group in which a stabilizer of two points contains no involutions, and a stabilizer of three points is trivial. Is it true that$G$is similar to$PGL_2(P)$in its natural action on the projective line$P \{ \}$, for some field$P$of characteristic~$2$? This is true under the condition that the stabilizer of two points is periodic.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
